actual_coverage.reasoning: Both ask for multi-year drivers and assumptions behind sustained growth (Perfecta targets, yields/ROIC) despite current headwinds; close but predicted focuses more on framework than pre-pandemic comparison.
Predicted asks for the quarterly yield trajectory and drivers, which aligns with probing Q2/Q3 softness and confidence in Q4 to reach the full-year yield guide.
Direct Med/3Q booking-curve quantification ask: remaining inventory, pace decline and rebound, and how it’s embedded in guidance.
Both target where bookings stand now versus earlier in the year, split close-in vs forward, and specifically visibility into 2027.
Predicted requests yield/APD uplift and ramp timing for Mexico/Cozumel and Beach Clubs, aligning with ramp cadence and structural yield opportunity; doesn’t explicitly address Galveston penetration.
Actual is a construction-status clarification for Mexico; predicted deals with capex/FCF cadence broadly, not the pause/resumption detail.
Both discuss forward-year implications of 2024 European headwinds and assumptions underpinning medium-term targets (recovery into 2027).
Direct yield-driver decomposition (new hardware, islands) and whether Europe grows—substantially the same as the predicted breakdown ask.
Actual asks about itinerary changes due to fuel and whether any spend is deferred; predicted focuses on cost cadence and Perfecta contributions—related cost area but different ask.
Predicted covers fuel EPS sensitivity and hedging cadence in a high-volatility environment, addressing the hedge-rolling aspect of the actual two-parter (though not the NCC ex-fuel scale threshold).
Both probe loyalty/credit card and repeat guest monetization impact on yields and spend with requested KPIs.
Actual is North America airfare sensitivity; predicted references air cost impacts in Med/Europe—same broad area (air costs affecting demand) but different region/ask.

missed_actual_themes: Construction/permitting status and resumption details for Perfect Day Mexico
Whether higher fuel prompted itinerary changes and whether initiatives/spend were deferred to later years
North America airfare sensitivity for North American itineraries and consumer behavior
Threshold at which NCC ex-fuel would start growing with higher capacity (scale limit) was not directly asked
overpredicted_themes: Capital allocation cadence (buybacks vs dividends vs debt paydown) and leverage triggers
Joint venture/TUI contribution outlook and drivers
Capex/FCF cadence and debt maturities/refinancing specifics
Industry pricing discipline and competitive behavior
Deep AI/A-B test details and granular pre-cruise category margin mix
summary: Predictions covered most core themes: Med/3Q booking dynamics and recovery, yield decomposition (incl. new hardware/destinations), booking visibility into 2027, loyalty/card monetization, and fuel hedging sensitivities. 9 of 12 actuals were substantially covered (avg best score ~2.92). Precision was solid (71/110 useful), though many predictions over-indexed on capital allocation, JV/TUI, and capex/FCF topics absent from the actual Q&A. Biggest misses were construction-status of Mexico, itinerary changes/deferrals due to fuel, and NA airfare sensitivity.
